title: Yunnanopilia longistaminea
taxonomy: Yunnanopilia longistaminea is a plant species within the family Hamamelidaceae. It belongs to the genus Yunnanopilia, which is a group of flowering plants. Yunnanopilia longistaminea is a part of the larger plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta. This plant is further classified into the phylum Angiospermophyta, which comprises flowering plants. The classification of Yunnanopilia longistaminea is based on its morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.

intro: Yunnanopilia longistaminea, also known as Yunnanopilia, is a plant species in the family Lauraceae, native to the subtropical regions of Yunnan province in China. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Yunnanopilia longistaminea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Yunnanopilia is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and reduce inflammation.
